//: Illustrative MCP server config — the shape AGENTD_MCP_CONFIG points at.
//1: Each server is launched over stdio (RFC 0004 §3.11): agentd spawns the
//2: command, speaks JSON-RPC 2.0 / NDJSON on its stdin+stdout, and drains
//3: stderr into structured logs. The launch argv is TRUSTED config and is
//4: NEVER built from model- or server-controlled strings (RFC 0012).
//5: On the CLI this same shape is expressed as --mcp name=command arg arg
//6: (repeatable). The config-file layer carries the argv as an explicit array.
//7: The optional `tools`/`resources` allowlists scope what a subagent may see
//8: and call (RFC 0009/0012); omit a field to allow all from that server.

servers.name: inbox
servers.//: Exposes subscribable resources so reactive mode can wake on new items.
